message:
fix(engine): correct FPR/FNR denominators, Laplacian multi-edge accumulation, p99 index, and closure binding API

Four independent bug fixes across engine modules:

parser_error_recovery.rs: Fix false-positive rate and false-negative rate
denominators in CalibrationReport. FPR should divide by actual negatives
(FP + TN), not predicted positives (TP + FP). FNR should divide by actual
positives (TP + FN), not predicted negatives (TN + FN). The previous
denominators were swapped, producing inverted error rates.

spectral_fleet_convergence.rs: Fix Laplacian matrix construction to
accumulate weights (-=) instead of overwriting (=) for off-diagonal
entries. When multiple edges exist between the same node pair, only the
last weight was recorded, corrupting the spectral decomposition.

hybrid_lane_router.rs: Fix p99 latency calculation to use
(len-1)*99/100 instead of len*99/100, preventing off-by-one that could
index past the actual 99th percentile. Also use config's conformal and
change_point settings in HybridLaneRouter::new() instead of defaults,
and remove redundant field reassignments in tests.

closure_model.rs: Pass name by value to new_hoisted() instead of
cloning, and use env.add_binding(slot) instead of direct map insertion
to go through the proper binding registration path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
